title: Restart and Update an App
summary: Fix common tech problems with a simple routine.
learnerPromise: Fix common tech problems with a simple routine.
whyItMatters: Devices sometimes freeze, crash, or slow down. Having a step-by-step routine saves frustration and often fixes the issue without needing outside help.
realLifeUses: An app keeps crashing when you open it.
Your device is much slower than usual.
A web page will not load even though you have Wi-Fi.
Sound or video is not working during a call.
youWillLearn: Restart an app or device the right way.
Check if an update is available and install it.
Test whether the problem is the app, the device, or the connection.
Know when to ask for help after trying the basics.
miniChallenge: Pick one app on your device and practice the restart-check-update routine.
steps: Close the app completely and reopen it.
If the problem continues, restart your entire device.
Go to your app store or settings and check for available updates.
If the issue is with one app, uninstall and reinstall it.
Test the app again. If it still does not work, note the exact error message for when you ask for help.
completionCriteria: You walked through the full restart-check-update-reinstall routine.
You can name which step helped or that the problem needs more support.
commonProblems: The device feels frozen — hold the power button down until it restarts.
You cannot find the update section — look in Settings under System or Software Update.
Reinstalling the app did not help — the issue may be with the app itself and not your device.
tips: Restarting fixes more problems than most people expect.
Check your internet connection first if only online features are broken.
Write down error messages exactly — they help when you talk to support.
